full_name: Stochastic Accelerated Gradient Descent (Composite, General Convex)
archetype_note: Uses a novel momentum-based update structure with three sequences and composite objective handling, distinct from plain SGD.
prerequisite_lib_work: Definition of Bregman divergence `bregman_divergence` and its strong convexity properties (Eq 3.2.1-3.2.3).
Definition of Stochastic First-Order Oracle `SFO` with unbiasedness and variance bounds (Assumption 2, 3).
Definition of generalized smoothness/strong convexity condition (Eq 4.2.2).
Definition of composite objective function `composite_objective` (\Psi = f + h).

space_geometry.description: Closed convex set $X \subset \mathbb{R}^m$ with norm $\|\cdot\|$ and dual norm $\|\cdot\|_*$. Distance generating function $\nu$ is strongly convex with modulus $\sigma_\nu$ (Eq 3.2.1). Bregman divergence $V(x, z)$ defined (Eq 3.2.2). Diameter $D_X^2 = \max V(x_1, x)$ (Eq 3.2.4).

oracle_properties.description: SFO returns $G(x, \xi)$ such that $\mathbb{E}[G(x, \xi)] = f'(x)$ (Eq 4.1.3) and variance $\mathbb{E}[\|G - f'\|_*^2] \le \sigma^2$ (Eq 4.1.4). Optionally, sub-Gaussian tails (Assumption 4, Eq 4.1.15).

objective_properties.description: \Psi(x) = f(x) + h(x). $f$ satisfies \mu V(x, y) \le f(y) - f(x) - \langle f'(x), y - x \rangle \le \frac{L}{2} \|y - x\|^2 + M \|y - x\| (Eq 4.2.2). $h$ is simple convex.
objective_properties.note: Covers smooth (L>0), nonsmooth (M>0), and strongly convex (\mu>0) cases.

update_rule.math: \underline{x}_t = (1 - \alpha_t) \bar{x}_{t-1} + \alpha_t x_{t-1}, \quad x_t = \arg\min_{x \in X} \{ \gamma_t [\langle G(\underline{x}_t, \xi_t), x \rangle + h(x)] + V(x_{t-1}, x) \}
update_rule.interpretation: Compute extrapolated point, then perform proximal step with stochastic gradient.
update_rule.note: General \mu > 0 case follows Prop 4.2 structure (Eq 4.2.5-4.2.7 referenced).

output.description: Weighted average sequence converging to optimum.
output.note: Convergence rate depends on stepsize policy.

3_key_lemma_chain.title: Key Lemma Chain
3_key_lemma_chain.overview: Proof relies on basic Mirror Descent inequality, one-step recursion for accelerated method, telescoping sum, and martingale concentration for high-probability bounds.

lemma_MD_basic.title: Basic Mirror Descent Inequality
lemma_MD_basic.statement_math: \gamma_t \langle g(x_t), x_{t+1} - x \rangle + V(x_t, x_{t+1}) \le V(x_t, x) - V(x_{t+1}, x)
proof_sketch: Apply three-point identity for Bregman divergence.

lemma_martingale.title: Martingale Concentration Bound
proof_sketch: Use exponential moment bound for sub-Gaussian variables.
Apply Markov inequality to exponential sum.

prop_one_step.title: One-Step Recursion
proof_sketch: Apply Lemma 3.5 (prox-lemma) to the update step.
Combine inequalities and define error term \Delta_t(x) (Eq 4.2.13).

prop_telescoping.title: Accumulated Inequality
proof_sketch: Use telescoping property of V terms.

interpretation.first_term: Initial distance to optimum scaled by stepsizes.
interpretation.second_term: Accumulated stochastic noise and nonsmoothness error.
interpretation.rate: Depends on stepsize policy (e.g. O(1/k^2) for smooth, O(1/sqrt(k)) for nonsmooth).

formalization_roadmap.phase_1: Define Bregman divergence, SFO, and generalized smoothness condition.
formalization_roadmap.phase_3: Prove Prop 4.2, Prop 4.3, and finally Theorem 4.4.
formalization_roadmap.note: Key blocker is managing the complex stepsize relations (Eq 4.2.9, 4.2.10, 4.2.22) in Lean.
